diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was reoriented based on the heterogeneous aspect of the lesion on MRI, peripheral contrast and inflammatory perilesional variation, and multiple peripheral infracentrimetrical vesicles seen on axial T2-weighted MRI. PET/CT scan confirmed the hepatic lesion with a central hypodense region and strong peripheral hypermetabolic rim. Microscopic examination of a liver biopsy showed necrosis and typical PAS-positive thin lamellar layer. Blood tests with Em2+ and Em18 ELISA and immunoblotting supported the diagnosis of AE.
